title: neonauclea parviflora
intro: Neonauclea parviflora, a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, pain, and inflammation, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Neonauclea parviflora include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Neonauclea parviflora is often used to make tea, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
